title: gardenia angkorensis
intro: Gardenia angkorensis, also known as the Cambodian gardenia, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible fruit. The medicinal properties of Gardenia angkorensis include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. This plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin infections, and promoting digestive health. In traditional herbal medicine, Gardenia angkorensis is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems.
